claims.claim_text: Article 4 of the AI Act has applied since 2 February 2025 and requires providers and deployers of AI systems to ensure a sufficient level of AI literacy of their staff and other persons acting on their behalf.
claims.notes: This is the narrow, official formulation. It is not a generic requirement for every employee in every company.

claims.claim_text: The AI literacy obligation already applies, while supervision and enforcement begin in August 2026.
claims.notes: The official Q&A uses both 3 August 2026 and 2 August 2026 wording; the safe formulation is 'August 2026', not a single hard day.

claims.claim_text: Luxembourg State support exists for business vocational training plans.
claims.notes: This is scheme-level training support, not proof that the proposed workshop is automatically eligible.

claims.claim_text: Luxembourg company-training financial aid is available to private-sector businesses legally established in Luxembourg, and it excludes mandatory continuing training for regulated professions.
claims.notes: Useful for routing, but product-level eligibility still requires a separate check.

claims.claim_text: Fit 4 AI is a real Luxembourg SME support route that offers co-financing for an AI assessment and roadmap phase, including 50% consultancy cost coverage.
claims.notes: This supports the existence of a public AI support route, but not direct reimbursement for the proposed workshop format.

claims.claim_text: Eurostat reported that 14.4% of Luxembourg enterprises with 10 or more employees used AI technologies in 2023.
claims.notes: This is the strongest public adoption statistic found for Luxembourg.

claims.claim_text: House of Training publicly offers a 4h face-to-face AI fundamentals course in English for 175 EUR.
claims.notes: This is direct evidence of a public Luxembourg AI literacy substitute.

claims.claim_text: House of Training publicly offers a 4h distance-learning EU AI Act course in English for 175 EUR, and its programme explicitly includes the framework and compliance implications of the EU AI Act.
claims.notes: This directly weakens any exclusivity claim around AI Act framing.

claims.claim_text: House of Training publicly offers a 4h AI risk, data protection, and artificial intelligence course in both face-to-face and distance-learning formats, with hands-on AI tool exercises.
claims.notes: This shows that practical AI + compliance/risk training already exists publicly in Luxembourg.

claims.claim_text: The AI literacy obligation becomes a compliance concern only by August 2026.
claims.notes: The obligation already applies from 2 February 2025; August 2026 is the enforcement window, not the start of the obligation.

claims.claim_text: This is the only Luxembourg offer combining EU AI Act compliance, hands-on practice, and audit-style documentation.
claims.notes: Public Luxembourg alternatives already combine AI basics, EU AI Act framing, risk/data-protection language, and hands-on exercises.

claims.claim_text: The provider-issued AI Literacy Certificate carries external compliance significance.
claims.notes: No public authority or regulator source was found confirming official significance for a provider-issued certificate.

claims.claim_text: Audit-ready documentation from this workshop is sufficient for compliance use.
claims.notes: No regulator or public scheme source confirms that this workshop's documentation would satisfy compliance or audit needs.
No primary public source confirming this exact figure was retained.

claims.claim_text: Fine exposure of up to 7.5 million EUR is a safe anchor for this product's compliance framing.
claims.notes: The specific fine anchor was not re-verified in this rerun and should not be used in the product narrative.

claims.claim_text: An SME AI package covering 70% up to 17,500 EUR applies to this exact workshop.
claims.notes: Public AI support exists, but workshop-level eligibility on those terms was not proven.

open_questions.question: Does the proposed workshop certificate have any official or buyer-recognised compliance significance beyond proof of attendance?
open_questions.reason: No public authority source confirmed external standing for the certificate.

open_questions.question: Can the workshop's documentation be marketed as compliance or audit evidence?
open_questions.reason: No regulator or public funding source confirmed that claim.

open_questions.question: Is this exact workshop eligible for Fit 4 AI or any AI-specific subsidy route, as opposed to general company-training co-funding?
open_questions.reason: Scheme-level support exists, but product-specific eligibility was not publicly proven.

open_questions.question: What differentiation remains once the exclusivity claim is removed and House of Training is treated as a verified competitor?
open_questions.reason: The remaining wedge likely depends on targeting, packaging, or customization rather than on a unique market gap.
